title: Well-definedness of chi^O(H) as a minimum not stated

comments.author: human
comments.body: The characteristic function is defined as $\chi^{\mathcal O}(H):=\min\{K\mid K\to H\}=\bigcap_{K\to H}K$. That the intersection actually lies in $\{K:K\to H\}$ (so the 'min' exists as a single element) needs justification. It does hold: if $K_1\to H$ and $K_2\to H$ then by closure under restriction ($K_2\subseteq H$) we get $K_1\cap K_2\to K_2$, and by transitivity of the partial order $K_1\cap K_2\to H$; hence the set is closed under intersection and the minimum equals the total intersection. This is low-severity but should appear as a one-line lemma, since the rest of the argument (e.g. 'minimal subgroup with $\chi(G)\to G$' in Lemma geom) relies on $\chi^{\mathcal O}(H)\to H$ being an actual arrow of $\mathcal O$.
